Verified against prompt instruction (split layout for Map and Polar radar view) which corresponds to layout 4 in GPredict.

Verified NORAD IDs via CelesTrak and Wikipedia. NORAD 43013 corresponds to NOAA-20 (JPSS-1), not GOES 16. GOES 16 is NORAD 41866. This contradiction exists both in the task prompt and the verifier logic.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real-world GPredict satellite data. Most target parameters strictly map to prompt instructions. However, a major defect exists in the task design: the prompt requests 'GOES 16 [43013]' for Geostationary visualization, and the verifier checks for NORAD ID 43013. In reality, and within GPredict's TLE database, 43013 is NOAA 20 (JPSS-1) which is a sun-synchronous/polar satellite. GOES 16's actual NORAD ID is 41866. If an agent searches for 'GOES 16' by name, they will configure the wrong ID (41866) and fail the strict verifier. Alternatively, if they search by ID 43013, they will configure JPSS-1 instead of GOES 16.
